source.title: Hazardous Ammonia Release at Cuisine Solutions, Inc. Facility

actions_taken: Site evacuation directed by production manager
4 workers admitted to hospitals, 1 placed in intensive care
Production lines shut down for 16 and 38 days respectively
event.summary: On July 31, 2024, at approximately 8:20 p.m., an emergency pressure relief valve on Heat Exchanger 5 Surge Drum in Tank Farm 5 opened due to overpressure, discharging approximately 275 pounds of anhydrous ammonia to the atmosphere. The discharged ammonia contained a high level of liquid, causing it to rapidly drop to ground level near the employee parking lot and building. During evacuation, 33 workers were injured by ammonia inhalation, with 4 admitted to hospitals. The incident was caused by overpressure in the vessel, failure to discharge to a safe location, and insufficient emergency preparedness including lack of effective alarms and emergency shutdown procedures.
recommendations: IIAR to update ANSI/IIAR 2 to include guidance for preventing or mitigating liquid or two-phase atmospheric discharges from emergency pressure relief systems
IIAR to update ANSI/IIAR 2 to require assessment of whether emergency pressure relief devices discharge to a safe location
Cuisine Solutions to reduce likelihood or mitigate consequences of liquid or two-phase atmospheric discharges through engineering controls and emergency pressure control systems
Cuisine Solutions to implement electronic process data historian and management system
Cuisine Solutions to update Emergency Action Plan with separate indoor/outdoor release procedures, evacuation routes, windsock guidance, and annual drills
Cuisine Solutions to add ammonia-specific alarm system with documented response procedures

hazards.name: Anhydrous ammonia inventory in refrigeration system
Heat exchanger surge drums containing saturated liquid ammonia

threats.name: Overpressure in Heat Exchanger 5 Surge Drum
Chilled water pump shutdown due to electrical cabinet overheating
Loss of cooling flow to heat exchanger
Liquid ammonia carryover in relief discharge

consequences.name: Toxic ammonia cloud formation at ground level
Serious injury to workers (respiratory damage, eye irritation)
Exposure to nearby public receptor (Postal Inspection Service)

controls.name: Emergency pressure relief valve on Heat Exchanger 5 Surge Drum
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overpressure by opening at MAWP
controls.lod_basis: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requirement

supporting_text: Each pressure vessel in Tank Farm 5 included at least one emergency pressure relief valve
The relief system assembly used in the surge drums included a three-way selector valve with two emergency pressure relief valves attached

controls.name: Common relief header discharge to atmosphere via tee diffuser
controls.barrier_role: Safely discharge relieved ammonia to atmosphere
controls.lod_basis: Standard practice for ammonia refrigeration systems

supporting_text: The atmospheric discharge was vented next to the employee parking lot and the building, which restricted the ability to safely evacuate, caused harm to people
A fundamental design principle for an emergency pressure relief system is to ensure that the system discharges to a safe location and no people are harmed

controls.name: Ammonia detection sensor system in production areas
controls.barrier_role: Detect ammonia release and alert personnel

supporting_text: Throughout the incident none of the alarms from the ammonia detection sensor system inside the production areas of the plant activated, and no sitewide evacuation alarm sounded

controls.name: Emergency shutdown buttons for refrigeration system
controls.barrier_role: Limit ammonia release by stopping refrigeration system

supporting_text: During the incident no one used the emergency shutdown buttons that could have limited the ammonia release
Not having written emergency shutdown procedures employees could follow before or during evacuation

controls.name: Emergency Action Plan (EAP)
controls.barrier_role: Guide safe evacuation and emergency response
controls.lod_basis: OSHA PSM and EPA RMP requirements

supporting_text: The CSB identified several gaps in the EAP and in the site's overall emergency preparedness, including: not accounting for wind direction; not differentiating between indoor and outdoor ammonia releases; not having written emergency shutdown procedures
The onsite personnel generally executed the site's Emergency Action Plan (EAP) as written. However, the CSB identified several gaps

controls.name: Refrigeration system monitoring by technician
controls.barrier_role: Detect process upsets and prevent overpressure

supporting_text: At approximately 8:17 p.m., the refrigeration technician returned to Tank Farm 5 and looked at all three heat exchangers in the refrigeration loop. The technician later told the CSB that, at that time, the ammonia pressures were in the normal operating range
The refrigeration technician then left the Sterling site for a meal break at approximately 8:19 p.m.
At approximately 8:20 p.m., an emergency pressure relief valve discharged to the atmosphere

controls.name: Chilled water pump thermal protection
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of cooling flow due to electrical overheating

supporting_text: The refrigeration technician believed the water pumps had shut down due to high temperature inside an outdoor electrical cabinet that was in direct afternoon sunlight
The refrigeration technician opened a door on the outdoor electrical cabinet and could feel the heat coming out of it

controls.name: Sensor in common relief header to detect leaking relief valves
controls.barrier_role: Alert technicians to relief valve activation or leakage

supporting_text: The common relief header also included a sensor to detect ammonia inside the piping. This would alert refrigeration technicians when an emergency pressure relief valve was leaking and required maintenance or had been activated in an overpressure event

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
